senses.definition: used to refer to a company, organization, etc. that is prosperous and developing rapidly

examples.x: Ever since the new CEO took charge, the company has been on its feet, experiencing significant growth and expanding into new markets.
The startup faced stiff competition from established players in the market, but it was able to remain on its feet by innovating and delivering a superior product.

senses.definition: to become successful again after experiencing difficulties or financial problems

examples.x: The city had suffered from a natural disaster, but with the help of government aid and community support, it was able to regain its feet and rebuild stronger than before.
The company had a rough year after losing a major client, but with a new marketing strategy and some key hires, they were able to regain their feet and start growing again.

senses.definition: to have achieved great success or made great progress

examples.x: I've come a long way since I started this job.
She came a long way from being a poor child to becoming a successful businesswoman.

senses.definition: to become more and more successful with the passage of time

examples.x: Over the years, their business has gone from strength to strength.
The team has gone from strength to strength, winning each match this season.
Her skills as a musician continue to go from strength to strength.
With each passing year, the company goes from strength to strength, expanding its market share.
After the initial success, the project went from strength to strength, gaining more supporters.

word_list.pronunciation: hæv ɐ lˈɑːŋ wˈeɪ tə ɡˈoʊ

senses.definition: to be in a place or position where one needs to make a lot of progress in order to achieve a desired result

examples.x: Despite working hard for months, the company had a long way to go to recover from the financial losses of the previous year.
If the athlete wants to compete at the highest level, she knows that she has a long way to go and will need to work on her technique and endurance.

senses.definition: to experience success or good fortune, particularly after a period of facing challenges or setbacks

examples.x: Despite ending a long-term relationship, Sarah landed on her feet by reconnecting with old friends and finding new hobbies that brought her joy and fulfillment.
Despite losing his job, Mark was able to land on his feet and find a better position with a different company.
Despite the initial setbacks, the new project faced, the team worked hard and managed to land on their feet with a successful product launch.
After a failed investment, Tom learned valuable lessons and eventually landed on his feet with a profitable business venture.
Following the company restructuring, many employees feared job loss, but fortunately, most of them landed on their feet with new opportunities within the organization.

senses.definition: to return to a position of success, influence or popularity after experiencing a decline or setback

examples.x: Despite being out of the public eye for several years, the singer made a comeback with a new album that received critical acclaim and commercial success.
The author was able to make a comeback after a series of poorly received books, with a new novel that became a bestseller and earned critical praise.

senses.definition: to make an effort to improve a situation after an unfortunate event or unexpected setback

examples.x: After the breakup of their band, the musicians had to pick up the pieces and start new projects to continue pursuing their passion for music.
The city had to pick up the pieces after a major storm caused widespread damage and power outages.

word_list.pronunciation: pˌʊt ɡɛt sˈɛt wˈʌnz hˈaʊs ɪn ˈɔːɹdɚ

senses.definition: to improve a situation or one's behavior for better results

examples.x: After a messy divorce and struggling as a single parent for a few years, Jane knew it was time to set her house in order and establish a more stable routine for herself and her children.
After her whirlwind romance, Susan decided it was time to put her house in order before settling down with her new partner.
After years of financial mismanagement, Liz realized she needed to get her house in order by paying down debts and creating a budget if she wanted to retire comfortably one day.

The recent college graduate realized she needed to set her house in order before taking on a challenging new job - that meant creating budgets, meal plans, and schedules to get organized and prepared.
The charity event was a roaring success, raising over a million dollars for their cause and bringing much needed attention and donors.
The concert was a roaring success, with a sold-out crowd of enthusiastic fans cheering on the band for encore after encore.
The product launch was a roaring success, with pre-orders exceeding even the most optimistic sales forecasts.

senses.definition: an unattractive or unsuccessful person or thing that later turns attractive or successful

examples.x: As a child, Jane was awkward and shy, but in her teenage years she blossomed into a beautiful young woman - a true "ugly duckling" story.
The small, struggling startup was once dismissed as an "ugly duckling", but after securing major funding and hiring top talent it's now seen as a rising star in the industry.
